title: handle_wallet_pay: payment request sent before session persisted
description: daemon/src/api.rs:329-350: tx.send(rpc_req) broadcasts PaymentAction::Request to the relay (and thus the peer) at line 329-337. Then wallet.upsert_session(&session).await is called at line 340. If upsert_session fails, the function returns 500 to the HTTP caller. However, the PaymentAction::Request has already been broadcast. The peer receives it and may respond with PaymentAction::Address — but the local daemon has no session record to match the response against. The payment conversation is permanently broken: the local wallet never sends funds, while the peer thinks payment is incoming. Fix: persist the session row to DB first, then send the relay message. If the relay send fails, roll back (delete) the session. This matches the correct failure mode: no session = no payment sent = consistent state.
